product_overview: Drexii is a live AI agent platform that unifies multiple integrations (Gmail, Slack, Notion, etc.) into a single chat/automation interface, using multi-model routing, chained automations, persistent memory, and a confirm-before-act safety layer. This PRD focuses on core logic modules to test and the API surface that exercises them.
core_goals: Provide secure, reliable authentication and session management for users and protect API endpoints from unauthorized actions.
Deliver robust AI conversation flows (threads & messages) with safe tool integration, prompt sanitization, and model routing by task complexity.
Enable reusable automations and workflows that can be created, executed, chained, and audited with clear logs and condition evaluation.
Store and surface user memory safely and include it in prompts in a predictable, well-formatted way.
Support third-party integrations per user with credential storage and runtime testing.
Ensure core library modules (sanitizer, task classifier, schema translator, tool-call parser, chain evaluator, memory formatter) behave correctly across happy and edge cases.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: User sign-in/out, session cookie issuance, session check, and account deletion via InsForge-compatible auth.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with body {email, password} -> Receive 200 with {ok: true, provider} and session cookie set
GET /api/auth/check with session cookie -> Receive 200 with {authenticated: true}
POST /api/auth/logout with session cookie -> Receive 200 with {ok: true} and session cookie cleared
POST /api/auth/login with missing email -> Receive 400 Missing email or password
POST /api/auth/delete-account with session cookie and body {userId} -> Receive 200 {ok: true}
POST /api/auth/delete-account without session or invalid userId -> Receive 400 Missing userId or 401 depending on auth state

features.name: Threads (Conversation Management)
features.description: Create conversation threads and retrieve thread data and messages for authenticated users.
user_flows: POST /api/threads with optional header x-user-id and body {title: 'Project X'} -> Receive 200 with {thread: Thread} (creates thread; x-user-id optional for unauthenticated ephemeral threads)
GET /api/threads with valid session or x-user-id -> Receive 200 with {threads: Thread[]} (lists threads for user)
GET /api/threads/:id with valid session/x-user-id -> Receive 200 with {thread: Thread, messages: Message[]}
POST /api/threads with empty body (no title) -> Receive 200 with created thread (title optional)
GET /api/threads/:id for non-existent id -> Receive 404 Thread not found
POST /api/threads without required auth when endpoint expects auth -> Receive 401

features.name: Messages / AI Chat
features.description: Post user messages to a thread, receive AI replies (buffered JSON) or SSE streaming replies while exercising sanitization, task classification, and tool-call parsing.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Summarize sales report'} -> Receive 200 with {message: Message, reply: Message} (AI reply returned, classifier used to pick model)
POST /api/threads/:id/messages?stream=true with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Long analysis...'} -> Receive 200 SSE stream of AI tokens until completion

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Call tool X'} -> Receive 200 with AI reply that includes [TOOL_CALL:ToolName({...})] and server parses tool call via parseToolCalls
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with empty content -> Receive 400 Missing content or thread id
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with header missing x-user-id -> Receive 401 User ID required
POST /api/threads/:id/messages when thread id not found -> Receive 404 Thread not found
POST /api/threads/:id/messages causing excessive requests -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded

features.name: Prompt Injection Sanitizer
features.description: Sanitize external tool outputs and wrap tool contexts so AI prompts do not inherit malicious instructions or prompt injections.
user_flows: POST /api/threads with header x-user-id and body {title: 'Tool test'} -> Receive 200 {thread}

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Use connected tool and include its output'} -> Receive 200 with {message, reply} where reply originally contained tool output; GET /api/threads/:id -> Receive 200 with messages where tool outputs have had prompt-injection patterns replaced with [FILTERED] (sanitizeToolOutput applied) -> (happy path demonstrating wrapToolContext when tools are included)

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: '' (empty)} -> Receive 400 Missing content or thread id (edge-case: sanitizer must handle empty/blank inputs without server error)

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with tool output containing multiple injection patterns -> Receive 200; GET /api/threads/:id -> Receive sanitized message where multiple patterns are replaced (case-insensitive handling)

features.name: AI Task Classifier (model-router)
features.description: Classify incoming user messages into 'lite' | 'standard' | 'heavy' | 'code' to route to appropriate AI model and settings.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Write a short email'} -> Receive 200 with {reply} and classifier returns 'standard' (model-router.classifyTask invoked)

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Here is a big research prompt with more than eighty words...'} -> Receive 200 with {reply} and classifier returns 'heavy' (word count rule applied)
POST /api/threads/:id/messages where last message is missing or not from user (server-side) -> Receive 200 with reply but classifier returns 'heavy' per rule 1 (edge-case handling)
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with content that contains code keywords 'function' and 'sql' -> Receive 200 with reply and classifier returns 'code'
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with empty messages array or empty content -> Receive 400 Missing content or thread id (classifier must handle empty arrays without throwing)

features.name: Tool Schema Translator
features.description: Translate internal tool schemas to Anthropic and Gemini formats used when invoking model tool APIs or generating function declarations.
user_flows: POST /api/workflows with headers {x-user-id} and body {name: 'Tooled Workflow', steps: [{toolSchema: {...}}]} -> Receive 200 with {workflow: Workflow} and server uses toAnthropicTools/toGeminiTools to generate tool declarations during workflow run
POST /api/workflows/:id/run with valid session and existing workflow id -> Receive 200 {result: string} where server used converted Anthropic/Gemini tool declarations to call models (happy path)
POST /api/workflows with body missing name -> Receive 400 Validation error (error flow)
POST /api/workflows with steps empty array -> Receive 200 with created workflow; toAnthropicTools/toGeminiTools should handle empty arrays (edge case)

features.name: Tool Call Parser
features.description: Extract and parse [TOOL_CALL: name(jsonArgs)] occurrences from AI responses; fall back to simple parsing when JSON.parse fails.
user_flows: POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Trigger tool call'} -> Receive 200 with {message, reply} where reply contains '[TOOL_CALL: send_email({"to":"x@x.com","subject":"Hi"})]' and server returns reply and records parsed tool call via parseToolCalls (happy path)

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Trigger malformed tool call'} -> Receive 200 with {message, reply, warnings} where reply contains '[TOOL_CALL: tool({to: me, subject: hi})]' and parseToolCalls falls back to simple key:value parsing or returns warning (graceful fallback)
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with AI output containing multiple tool calls in one string -> Receive 200 and server returns reply with an array of parsed tool calls (multiple-handling)
POST /api/threads/:id/messages where tool call args are completely unparseable -> Receive 200 with reply and server logs/skips the tool call, returning a parse warning (no throw)

features.name: Chain Condition Evaluator
features.description: Evaluate plain-English chain conditions when deciding whether to trigger chained automations based on previous run outputs.
user_flows: POST /api/automations with headers {x-user-id} and body {name: 'Parent', trigger: 'schedule', instructions: '...', chainOn: 'child-id', triggerCondition: 'success'} -> Receive 200 {automation}

POST /api/automations/process with body {automationId: 'parent-id', context: '...'} -> Receive 200 {results: object[]} where evaluateChainCondition('success', output, 'success') returned true and child automation was queued/executed (happy path)

POST /api/automations/process with body {automationId: 'parent-id', context: '...'} where parent returned status 'error' but condition was 'success' -> Receive 200 {results: object[]} where child was NOT executed (condition evaluated to false)
POST /api/automations/process repeatedly exceeding limits -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded (error flow for rate-limiting)
POST /api/automations/process with null condition or 'always' -> Receive 200 and chain executes regardless of status (rule handling)

features.name: Automations (CRUD & Processing)
features.description: Create, list, delete, toggle automations, fetch logs, and process scheduled or manual automation runs.
user_flows: POST /api/automations with headers {x-user-id} and body {name: 'Daily Summary', trigger: 'schedule', instructions: 'Summarize today', triggerConfig: {cron: '0 8 * * *'}} -> Receive 200 with {automation: Automation}
GET /api/automations with valid session -> Receive 200 with {automations: Automation[]}
POST /api/automations/:id/toggle with valid session -> Receive 200 with {automation: Automation} (active toggled)
GET /api/automations/:id/logs with valid session -> Receive 200 with {logs: AutomationLog[]}
DELETE /api/automations/:id with valid session -> Receive 200 {ok: true}
POST /api/automations with missing name or invalid trigger -> Receive 400 Validation error (error flow)
POST /api/automations/process with body {automationId: 'non-existent'} -> Receive 200 {results: []} or appropriate error; repeated/process abuse -> Receive 429 Rate limit exceeded

features.name: User Integrations
features.description: Add, list, remove, and test third-party integration credentials per user (Notion, Slack, etc.).
user_flows: POST /api/user-integrations with headers {x-user-id} and body {integration: 'notion', credentials: {token: 'x'}} -> Receive 200 {integration: UserIntegration}
GET /api/user-integrations with valid session -> Receive 200 {integrations: UserIntegration[]}
POST /api/user-integrations/test with valid session and body {id: 'integration-id'} -> Receive 200 {ok: true, message: 'Connected'} (test call performed)
DELETE /api/user-integrations/:id with valid session -> Receive 200 {ok: true}
POST /api/user-integrations with missing credentials -> Receive 400 Validation error (error flow)

features.name: Memory (store & include in prompts)
features.description: Create, list, update, delete memory entries and format them for inclusion in AI prompts.
user_flows: POST /api/memory with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Prefers emails in morning', category: 'preference'} -> Receive 200 {memory: Memory}
GET /api/memory with valid session -> Receive 200 {memories: Memory[]}
PATCH /api/memory/:id with headers {x-user-id} and body {content: 'Prefers emails at 9am'} -> Receive 200 {memory: Memory}
DELETE /api/memory/:id with headers {x-user-id} -> Receive 200 {ok: true}
POST /api/memory with missing content -> Receive 400 Validation error (error flow)

POST /api/threads/:id/messages with headers {x-user-id} where user has memories -> Receive 200 where server included formatted memories wrapped in <memory_context> (formatMemoriesForPrompt applied; empty memory list -> '' edge-case)

features.name: Pending Actions (Confirm / Cancel)
features.description: List pending actions generated by AI that require user confirmation; confirm executes the action, cancel aborts it.
user_flows: GET /api/actions/pending with valid session -> Receive 200 {actions: Action[]} (list pending items)
POST /api/actions/:id/confirm with valid session -> Receive 200 {ok: true, result: string} (action executed)
POST /api/actions/:id/cancel with valid session -> Receive 200 {ok: true} (action canceled)
POST /api/actions/:id/confirm for non-existent id -> Receive 404 or 200 with error message depending on implementation (error flow)

features.name: Model Status
features.description: Expose current AI model providers and availability for routing decisions.
user_flows: GET /api/model/status -> Receive 200 with {models: object, active: string} showing providers (e.g., Claude, Gemini) and their health
GET /api/model/status when a provider is down -> Receive 200 with provider marked unavailable (graceful reporting)

features.name: Audit Log
features.description: Retrieve recent integration/tool execution audit trail for debugging and compliance.
user_flows: GET /api/audit with valid session -> Receive 200 {logs: AuditLog[]} (list recent audit entries)
GET /api/audit without auth -> Receive 401 (error flow)

tech_stack.other: Anthropic Claude AI, Google Generative AI, drizzle-kit

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Login/logout and session management via InsForge; session cookie set on success

endpoints.description: Authenticate user with email/password, set session cookie
Clear session cookie and sign out
Check if the current session is authenticated
Delete the current user account

features.name: Threads
features.description: Create and retrieve AI conversation threads

endpoints.description: Create a new conversation thread
List all threads for the authenticated user
Get a single thread with its messages

features.name: Messages / AI Chat
features.description: Post messages to a thread and get AI responses (buffered JSON or SSE stream)

endpoints.description: Send a user message and receive an AI reply; supports ?stream=true for SSE

features.name: Workflows
features.description: CRUD and execution of named AI workflows

endpoints.description: List all workflows for the user
Create a new workflow
Delete a workflow by id
Update a workflow
Execute a workflow by running its prompt through the AI

features.name: Automations
features.description: Create and manage AI automations with triggers (schedule, email, webhook, chain)

endpoints.description: List all automations for the user
Create a new automation
Delete an automation
Toggle automation active/inactive
Get execution logs for an automation
Process due scheduled automations or manually trigger one by automationId

features.name: User Integrations
features.description: Manage per-user third-party integration credentials (Notion, Slack, etc.)

endpoints.description: List user's connected integrations
Add a new integration credential
Remove an integration
Test if a saved integration credential is functional

features.name: Memory
features.description: Store and retrieve AI memory entries per user

endpoints.description: List memory entries for the user
Create a new memory entry
Delete a memory entry
Update a memory entry

features.name: Pending Actions
features.description: Approve or cancel AI-initiated write actions that require user confirmation

endpoints.description: List all pending actions awaiting confirmation
Confirm and execute a pending action
Cancel a pending action

features.name: Model Status
features.description: Report the current AI model provider status

endpoints.description: Get active AI model provider and availability

features.name: Audit Log
features.description: Retrieve integration tool execution audit trail

endpoints.description: List recent audit log entries

known_limitations.issue: x-user-id header is caller-supplied with no JWT verification — any caller can impersonate any userId
known_limitations.impact: Authorization relies on client honesty; no server-side token validation

known_limitations.issue: Session cookie is set but not validated in most protected endpoints (they check x-user-id header instead)
known_limitations.impact: Session cookie and x-user-id are decoupled; misuse possible

known_limitations.issue: Rate limiting is in-memory and resets on server restart
known_limitations.impact: Distributed deployments or restarts reset rate limit counters
